sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, values_list LIST<INTEGER>, description VARCHAR);
SELECT alias(values_list) AS value_alias, COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE values_list @> [2, NULL] GROUP BY alias(values_list);
SELECT alias(a.values_list) AS list_alias, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table b WHERE b.values_list @> a.values_list) FROM test_table a HAVING list_alias IS NOT NULL;
SELECT alias(values_list) AS list_alias, SUM(id) OVER (PARTITION BY list_alias) FROM test_table WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE values_list @> [1, 2]) > 0 ORDER BY list_alias;
CREATE TABLE another_table (item_id INTEGER, item_list LIST<INTEGER>, item_desc VARCHAR);
SELECT alias(a.item_list) AS item_list_alias, COUNT(*) FROM another_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.item_list @> [1, 2] WHERE b.id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY item_list_alias;
SELECT alias(item_list) AS product_list, AVG(id) FROM another_table WHERE item_list @> [3] HAVING AVG(id) > 1.5;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, alias(values_list) FROM test_table WHERE values_list IS NOT NULL;
SELECT alias(values_list) AS temp_list_alias, MIN(id) FROM temp_table WHERE temp_list_alias IS NOT NULL GROUP BY temp_list_alias;
